mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and supports equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate remains in the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2022-01-15 indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism triggers when unemployment rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 626833M and HOUST at 1679K as of 2022-01-15
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods when both RSAFS and HOUST show month-over-month growth, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below its 3-month moving average, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflationary pressures. This mechanism triggers when INDPRO falls more than 1.5% below its 3-month MA, predicting negative S&P 500 returns as investors reprice earnings and inflation risk over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite supply weakness, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, creating a negative real rate environment. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate imminent monetary tightening and repricing of equity risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 280.19 and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2022-01-15
regime_hint: inflation acceleration with accommodative policy lag
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month, signaling a slowing labor market that precedes broader economic weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors anticipate earnings compression from reduced consumer spending.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances despite weak payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows contraction or growth below 1% annualized, indicating economic stagnation. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward in response to weak aggregate demand.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods with weak GDP growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, signaling synchronized supply and labor market weakness that indicates imminent recession. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors flee equities in response to deteriorating economic fundamentals.
evidence_refs: fact:Both series available monthly as of 2022-01-15
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 62% of periods when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline month-over-month, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
